studies.title:
Evaluation of personalized cognitive home training using the 
mobile application Neuronation in adults with ADHD

_verification.reason: The study specifically evaluates the effectiveness of the 'NeuroNation MED' app, developed by Synaptikon GmbH, for cognitive training in adults with ADHD. The product name 'NeuroNation MED' and the manufacturer are explicitly mentioned in the full text.

_classification.reason: The study involves random allocation of participants into two groups: one receiving the intervention (Neuronation app) and the other serving as a control (TAU group), which is a hallmark of a randomized controlled trial.

studies.title: Evaluation of an app-based independent cognitive training - effects on cognitive performance and depression in patients with depression

_verification.reason: The study specifically investigates the effectiveness of the NeuroNation MED app, as it is explicitly named multiple times in the full text as the intervention being evaluated for cognitive training in patients with depression.

_classification.reason: The study is described as a randomized controlled trial, where participants are randomly allocated to one of three groups to evaluate the effectiveness of an app-based cognitive training program. Randomization and control groups are key features of an RCT.

studies.title: Evaluation of a mobile application for independent cognitive training – effects on cognitive abilities and quality of life in patients with Parkinson’s disease (PD) and mild cognitive impairment (MCI)

_verification.reason: The study specifically evaluates the 'NeuroNation MED' application for cognitive training in patients with Parkinson's disease and mild cognitive impairment. The product name 'NeuroNation MED' is explicitly mentioned in the trial description.

_classification.reason: The study involves random assignment of participants to a training group or a control group, which is a defining feature of a randomized controlled trial (RCT).

studies.title: Study for the evaluation of a mobile application for independent cognitive training - effects on cognitive abilities and quality of life of patients with mild cognitive disorders

_verification.reason: The study specifically investigates the effectiveness of the training app 'NeuroNation MED' for patients with mild cognitive disorders, as indicated in the trial registration and full text.

_classification.reason: The study is described as a randomized controlled trial investigating the effectiveness of a cognitive training app, with participants randomly allocated to an intervention group using the app or a control group receiving standard care or no care.
